title: Wycinanki — Polish folk paper-cutting
sources: General Polish folk-art tradition — PD by antiquity; widely documented in craft literature
notes: The Kurpie and Lowicz traditions of Polish wycinanki: bright layered papercuts assembled from multiple colours. The Kurpie form is a single-colour symmetrical rosette (fold-and-cut); the Lowicz form is multicoloured strips and geometric shapes layered over a base. Tutorial focuses on the Lowicz layered form — making the base circle, cutting cockerel or flower motifs in contrasting colours, layering and gluing. Scope: the craft of cutting and layering.
